metadata.description: Evaluates employer procedural compliance under ADA Title I after an accommodation request is made. Covers: (1) interactive process — mechanical compliance and good-faith quality; (2) medical inquiry limitations at each employment stage; (3) confidentiality of medical information. This module does not re-evaluate whether accommodation is required (Module 2). It evaluates how the employer handled the process and whether that handling was legally compliant.
metadata.legal_domain: Americans with Disabilities Act — Title I (Employment)

dependency_inputs.description: Fields consumed from Module 1 (ada_coverage_eligibility) and Module 2 (ada_accommodation_analysis) outputs. Module 3 does not re-evaluate these determinations but uses them to scope component execution.

is_covered_employer.name: is_covered_employer
is_covered_employer.description: If false, Module 3 components are not applicable. Medical inquiry and confidentiality obligations arise only under a covered entity.

is_covered_individual.name: is_covered_individual
is_covered_individual.description: If false, interactive process obligations do not attach. Medical inquiry limitations still apply to pre-offer applicants regardless of disability status.

accommodation_required.name: accommodation_required
accommodation_required.description: Module 2 composite output. If 'no', interactive process compliance is still evaluated to assess whether the employer's process leading to denial was procedurally sound. If 'indeterminate', interactive process evaluation proceeds but may itself be indeterminate.

interactive_process.description: Evaluates employer compliance with the informal interactive process obligation following an accommodation request. Produces three outputs: (3.1a) process_mechanically_compliant — rule-based binary check on procedural steps; (3.1b) process_quality — standards-based good-faith engagement assessment; (3.1c) interactive_process_compliant — composite combining 3.1a and 3.1b. Legal note: No specific formal process or written procedures are required by statute or regulation. The interactive process obligation derives from EEOC enforcement posture and case law, not an explicit regulatory mandate. Mechanical compliance checks reflect EEOC enforcement expectations, not explicit regulatory mandates.
interactive_process.source_citation: EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § General Principles; 29 CFR § 1630 Appendix
interactive_process.interactive_process_legal_basis_note: The interactive process is not expressly mandated by 42 U.S.C. § 12112 or 29 CFR Part 1630 as a formal procedural requirement. Its legal significance derives from EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 and judicial interpretation of 29 CFR § 1630 Appendix. Mechanical compliance checks in component 3.1a are operationalized EEOC enforcement expectations, labeled accordingly throughout logic rules.

request_acknowledged.name: request_acknowledged
request_acknowledged.description: True if the employer acknowledged receipt of the accommodation request and communicated next steps to the employee.

dialogue_initiated_date.name: dialogue_initiated_date
dialogue_initiated_date.description: Date the employer first initiated substantive interactive dialogue with the employee. Used to calculate business days elapsed from accommodation_request_date.

accommodation_request_date.name: accommodation_request_date
accommodation_request_date.description: Date the accommodation request was received or the employer first became aware of the need. Passed through from Module 2 input.

business_days_to_initiation.name: business_days_to_initiation
business_days_to_initiation.description: Number of business days elapsed between accommodation_request_date and dialogue_initiated_date. If not provided and both dates are present, the engine uses a calendar calculation. The 10-business-day timeliness heuristic is engine-defined, not a statutory deadline. See rule R048.

accommodation_options_documented.name: accommodation_options_documented
accommodation_options_documented.description: True if the employer documented the scope of accommodation options it considered during the interactive process.

employer_initiated_dialogue_promptly.name: employer_initiated_dialogue_promptly
employer_initiated_dialogue_promptly.description: True if the employer initiated dialogue without undue delay after the request. Relevant to process_quality factors_for analysis.

multiple_alternatives_considered.name: multiple_alternatives_considered
multiple_alternatives_considered.description: True if the employer considered more than one accommodation alternative before making a decision. Relevant to process_quality factors_for analysis.

jan_or_equivalent_consulted.name: jan_or_equivalent_consulted
jan_or_equivalent_consulted.description: True if the employer consulted the Job Accommodation Network or an equivalent disability resource during the accommodation analysis. Relevant to process_quality factors_for analysis.

decision_communicated_with_explanation.name: decision_communicated_with_explanation
decision_communicated_with_explanation.description: True if the employer communicated its accommodation decision with a reasoned explanation, whether granting or denying. Relevant to process_quality factors_for analysis.

follow_up_on_implemented_accommodation.name: follow_up_on_implemented_accommodation
follow_up_on_implemented_accommodation.description: True if the employer followed up after implementing the accommodation to confirm effectiveness. Relevant to process_quality factors_for analysis.

employer_delayed_without_explanation.name: employer_delayed_without_explanation
employer_delayed_without_explanation.description: True if the employer delayed the interactive process without providing an explanation to the employee. Relevant to process_quality factors_against analysis.

all_options_rejected_without_rationale.name: all_options_rejected_without_rationale
all_options_rejected_without_rationale.description: True if the employer rejected all identified accommodation options without documented rationale. Relevant to process_quality factors_against analysis.

relied_on_disability_stereotypes.name: relied_on_disability_stereotypes
relied_on_disability_stereotypes.description: True if the employer's accommodation assessment relied on generalizations or stereotypes about the disability rather than an individualized assessment of this employee. Relevant to process_quality factors_against analysis.

process_terminated_unilaterally.name: process_terminated_unilaterally
process_terminated_unilaterally.description: True if the employer terminated the interactive process before reaching a conclusion, without good cause and without the employee's voluntary departure from the process. Relevant to process_quality factors_against analysis.

employee_interactive_process_participati.name: employee_interactive_process_participation
employee_interactive_process_participati.description: The employee's level of participation in the interactive process. When refused or abandoned AND employer has documented basis, this is a weighting factor in process_quality — it shifts the output one level in the employer's favor but does not automatically produce process_quality = yes. It does not affect process_mechanically_compliant.

employer_documented_employee_nonparticip.name: employer_documented_employee_nonparticipation
employer_documented_employee_nonparticip.description: True if the employer has documented the basis for characterizing the employee's participation as refused or abandoned. Required for the non-participation weighting factor to apply under R053.

process_mechanically_compliant.name: process_mechanically_compliant
process_mechanically_compliant.description: True if the employer completed all three procedural steps: (1) acknowledged the request, (2) initiated dialogue within the engine-defined 10-business-day heuristic, and (3) documented accommodation options considered. All three must pass for true. This is an EEOC enforcement expectation, not a statutory mandate.
process_mechanically_compliant.source_citation: EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002; 29 CFR § 1630 Appendix

mechanical_compliance_checks.name: mechanical_compliance_checks
mechanical_compliance_checks.description: Granular results for each of the three procedural step checks.

acknowledged_request.name: acknowledged_request
acknowledged_request.description: True if employer acknowledged the accommodation request.

initiated_dialogue_timely.name: initiated_dialogue_timely
initiated_dialogue_timely.description: True if employer initiated dialogue within 10 business days of the request (engine-defined heuristic, not a statutory deadline).

documented_options_considered.name: documented_options_considered
documented_options_considered.description: True if employer documented the scope of accommodation options considered.

process_quality.name: process_quality
process_quality.description: Standards-based assessment of good-faith engagement in the interactive process. Evaluates factors for and against good faith. Employee non-participation (refused/abandoned with documentation) shifts this output one level in employer's favor but does not automatically produce yes.
process_quality.source_citation: EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § General Principles; 29 CFR § 1630 Appendix

process_quality_analysis.name: process_quality_analysis
process_quality_analysis.description: Factor analysis supporting the process_quality determination.

factors_for.name: factors_for
factors_for.description: Factors supporting a finding of good-faith engagement. Populated in alphabetical order.

factors_against.name: factors_against
factors_against.description: Factors against a finding of good-faith engagement. Populated in alphabetical order.

key_uncertainties.name: key_uncertainties
key_uncertainties.description: Specific information gaps affecting the process_quality determination. Required when process_quality is indeterminate.

nonparticipation_weighting_applied.name: nonparticipation_weighting_applied
nonparticipation_weighting_applied.description: True if the employee non-participation weighting factor was applied under R053, shifting process_quality one level in employer's favor.

interactive_process_compliant.name: interactive_process_compliant
interactive_process_compliant.description: Composite determination combining process_mechanically_compliant and process_quality per the combination matrix: (false + no = no), (false + indeterminate = indeterminate), (true + no = indeterminate — mechanical compliance does not cure bad faith), (true + yes = yes), all other combinations = indeterminate.
interactive_process_compliant.source_citation: EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002; 29 CFR § 1630 Appendix

interactive_process_risk_level.name: interactive_process_risk_level
interactive_process_risk_level.description: Ordinal risk rating derived from interactive_process_compliant and the presence of bad-faith factors.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of all three sub-determinations with source citations.

sources_cited.name: sources_cited
sources_cited.description: Source citations supporting the component determination.

medical_inquiry_limitations.description: Evaluates whether employer medical inquiries and examinations complied with ADA rules at each stage of employment. Three stages are evaluated independently: pre-offer, post-offer/pre-employment, and during-employment. Only stages where an inquiry occurred are evaluated. Each stage produces an inquiry_permitted determination, violation_basis if prohibited, and source citation.

inquiry_stage.name: inquiry_stage
inquiry_stage.description: The employment stage at which the inquiry occurred. One or more stages may be evaluated per API call.

inquiry_type.name: inquiry_type
inquiry_type.description: The type of inquiry or examination conducted.

inquiry_description.name: inquiry_description
inquiry_description.description: Freeform description of the specific inquiry or examination conducted. Used to populate reasoning output; does not affect logic.

post_offer_all_employees_examined.name: post_offer_all_employees_examined
post_offer_all_employees_examined.description: True if all entering employees in the same job category are subject to the same medical examination requirement. Required condition for post-offer examinations to be permissible under 42 U.S.C. § 12112(d)(3)(A).

post_offer_information_kept_confidential.name: post_offer_information_kept_confidential
post_offer_information_kept_confidential.description: True if medical information obtained at the post-offer stage is maintained on separate, confidential forms and in separate files. Required for post-offer examinations to be permissible under 42 U.S.C. § 12112(d)(3)(B).

post_offer_results_used_only_as_permitte.name: post_offer_results_used_only_as_permitted
post_offer_results_used_only_as_permitte.description: True if medical examination results are used only as permitted by the ADA — e.g., not to discriminate on the basis of disability unless a direct threat or qualification standard exception applies. Required for post-offer examinations to be permissible under 42 U.S.C. § 12112(d)(3)(C).

during_employment_inquiry_job_related.name: during_employment_inquiry_job_related
during_employment_inquiry_job_related.description: True if the inquiry or examination is job-related. Required for during-employment inquiries under 42 U.S.C. § 12112(d)(4)(A), unless the wellness program exception applies.

during_employment_inquiry_business_neces.name: during_employment_inquiry_business_necessity
during_employment_inquiry_business_neces.description: True if the inquiry or examination is consistent with business necessity. Required for during-employment inquiries under 42 U.S.C. § 12112(d)(4)(A), unless the wellness program exception applies.

during_employment_voluntary_wellness.name: during_employment_voluntary_wellness
during_employment_voluntary_wellness.description: True if the examination is part of a voluntary employee health program (wellness program). Voluntary wellness program examinations are excepted from the job-related and business necessity requirements under 42 U.S.C. § 12112(d)(4)(B).

multiple_inquiry_stages.name: multiple_inquiry_stages
multiple_inquiry_stages.description: Array of inquiry stage records when multiple stages are evaluated in a single API call. Each object mirrors the single-stage input structure (inquiry_stage, inquiry_type, and relevant conditional fields for that stage). When populated, this field drives multi-stage evaluation; single-stage fields above are ignored.

inquiry_evaluations.name: inquiry_evaluations
inquiry_evaluations.description: One evaluation object per inquiry stage evaluated. Each object contains the fields below.

inquiry_permitted.name: inquiry_permitted
inquiry_permitted.description: Yes if the inquiry or examination is permitted at this stage under ADA Title I. No if it violates the applicable prohibition.

violation_basis.name: violation_basis
violation_basis.description: Populated when inquiry_permitted is no. Describes the specific prohibition violated.

source_citation.name: source_citation
source_citation.description: Specific statutory and regulatory subsection supporting this stage's determination.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the inquiry determination for this stage.

medical_inquiry_compliant.name: medical_inquiry_compliant
medical_inquiry_compliant.description: True if all evaluated stages returned inquiry_permitted = yes. False if any stage returned inquiry_permitted = no.

medical_inquiry_risk_level.name: medical_inquiry_risk_level
medical_inquiry_risk_level.description: Ordinal risk rating. Pre-offer violations are critical (per se prohibition). Post-offer violations with improper use are high. During-employment violations without business necessity are moderate to high.

sources_cited.name: sources_cited
sources_cited.description: All source citations supporting the component determination.

confidentiality.description: Evaluates whether medical information obtained during the employment process was handled in accordance with ADA confidentiality requirements. Three binary checks are evaluated: (1) medical information maintained on separate forms and in separate files; (2) access limited to permitted parties only; (3) no unauthorized disclosure. All three must pass for confidentiality_compliant = yes.

medical_info_in_separate_file.name: medical_info_in_separate_file
medical_info_in_separate_file.description: True if medical information is collected and maintained on separate forms and in separate files from general personnel records.

access_limited_to_permitted_parties.name: access_limited_to_permitted_parties
access_limited_to_permitted_parties.description: True if access to medical information is restricted to: (a) supervisors and managers who need to know about work restrictions or accommodations; (b) first aid and safety personnel if the disability might require emergency treatment; (c) government officials investigating ADA compliance. All other access is unauthorized.

unauthorized_disclosure_occurred.name: unauthorized_disclosure_occurred
unauthorized_disclosure_occurred.description: True if medical information was disclosed to parties outside the three permitted categories. True value triggers a confidentiality violation.

supervisor_access_limited_to_restriction.name: supervisor_access_limited_to_restrictions_only
supervisor_access_limited_to_restriction.description: True if supervisors and managers who were given access received only information about necessary work restrictions or accommodations, not full medical records or diagnosis information. Supports the permitted-parties check.

disclosure_parties.name: disclosure_parties
disclosure_parties.description: List of parties to whom medical information was disclosed, if unauthorized_disclosure_occurred is true. Used to populate reasoning output.

confidentiality_checks.name: confidentiality_checks
confidentiality_checks.description: Individual binary check results for each of the three confidentiality requirements.

separate_file_maintained.name: separate_file_maintained
separate_file_maintained.description: True if medical information is on separate forms in separate files from personnel records.

access_properly_limited.name: access_properly_limited
access_properly_limited.description: True if access is limited to permitted parties only.

no_unauthorized_disclosure.name: no_unauthorized_disclosure
no_unauthorized_disclosure.description: True if no unauthorized disclosures occurred. False when unauthorized_disclosure_occurred input is true.

confidentiality_compliant.name: confidentiality_compliant
confidentiality_compliant.description: True only if all three confidentiality checks pass. False if any check fails.

violated_requirements.name: violated_requirements
violated_requirements.description: Populated when confidentiality_compliant is false. One entry per failed check.

description.name: description
description.description: Plain-language description of what requirement was violated.

confidentiality_risk_level.name: confidentiality_risk_level
confidentiality_risk_level.description: Ordinal risk rating. Unauthorized disclosure to external parties = critical. Improper access to coworkers = high. File commingling without disclosure = moderate.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of each check result with specific subsection citations.

sources_cited.name: sources_cited
sources_cited.description: Source citations supporting the component determination.
